company_location: Multiple locations, Germany (Stuttgart / Berlin / Bremen / Göppingen / Karlsruhe / München)
contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter was visible on the public TeamViewer careers posting checked on 2026-04-12, so the letter uses Hiring Team as the fallback.
availability_override: I am available to start from 26 April 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week during Summer Semester 2026. If there is an opportunity to continue in a full-time internship capacity later, I would also be open to that from 01 October 2026, at the start of Winter Semester 2026/27.
why_company: What caught my attention about TeamViewer is that this role stays very close to the real work of product management: competitor research, market analysis, workshops, requirements, knowledge sharing, and follow-through across teams. I am especially drawn to roles where product work is not abstract, but connected to day-to-day operations, structured communication, and practical delivery in a strong B2B software environment.
job_description: TeamViewer Germany GmbH is hiring a Work Student in Product Management for TeamViewer ONE across multiple German locations. The role supports product managers in day-to-day product operations for SMB and Enterprise use cases, including competitor research, market analysis, management presentations, participation in agile product rituals, support for product meetings and workshops, maintenance of a globally active internal knowledge community, preparation of product requirements and customer feedback topics, operational coordination, and ownership of smaller initiatives from idea to implementation. The posting asks for an enrolled student in business administration, business informatics, industrial engineering, international management, product/project management, marketing, computer science, or a related field, strong interest in B2B SaaS and enterprise technology, PowerPoint and Excel proficiency, strong communication, and fluent English; German is a plus.
requirements: Enrolled student in business administration, business informatics, industrial engineering, international management, product or project management, marketing, computer science, or a related field
Looking for practical product-management experience alongside studies
Strong interest in B2B software products, SaaS platforms, and enterprise technology
High affinity for software products and digital platforms
Very good proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint and Excel
Ability to work in cross-functional teams while managing tasks independently
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Fluency in English; German is a plus
cv_about_override: Product delivery, business analysis, and AI workflow professional focused on structured product operations, stakeholder alignment, and practical execution in complex digital environments.
cv_summary_override: Experienced product and business analysis professional with a strong foundation in research, requirements, stakeholder alignment, rollout, and continuous improvement. Alongside my earlier product background, I have also been building a broader profile in AI workflows, automation, and structured digital operations. This makes me well suited for working student roles that combine product support, market and customer analysis, cross-functional coordination, and practical follow-through in B2B software environments.
cover_letter_opening: I wanted to apply for the Work Student, Product Management (all genders) role at TeamViewer because the work itself caught my attention immediately. It stays close to the kind of product work I enjoy most: competitor research, market analysis, workshops, requirements, and practical follow-through across teams.
cover_letter_body_one: That kind of role fits how I have usually contributed in previous positions. A lot of my work has already been in B2B or B2B2C environments. SCCK, for example, was a B2B2C platform where supplier onboarding, trust, and operational workflows mattered more than consumer-style product thinking. At Anduin, I worked on a heavy B2B onboarding product for private markets, where product decisions had to stay aligned with legal, signing, and client-process constraints.
cover_letter_body_two: Talosix sharpened this side of my profile further. It was a B2B healthcare product serving hospitals and institutions in the US and Canada, so the work was closely tied to clinical workflows, operational reliability, and structured coordination across stakeholders. That background feels relevant for TeamViewer because I am comfortable in product roles where the real value comes from understanding business use cases clearly and helping teams translate them into practical execution.
cover_letter_motivation: I am particularly interested in TeamViewer because it is a strong B2B software company where product management sits close to real customer use cases, enterprise needs, and cross-functional execution. That is exactly the type of environment where I believe I can contribute while continuing to grow.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 26 April 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week during Summer Semester 2026. If there is an opportunity to continue in a full-time internship capacity later, I would also be open to that from 01 October 2026, at the start of Winter Semester 2026/27. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can support your product management team.
